task_updates.executor_notes: Verified `GenerationsPaneGallery.tsx` already uses `useGallerySelection()` and forwards both `selectGalleryItem` and `selectGalleryItems`; at lines 72-78 the context methods are read, at line 86 lasso selection forwards `selectGalleryItems`, and at lines 96-98 single-click selection forwards `selectGalleryItem(..., { toggle: modifierKeys.isMultiSelectModifier })`. Confirmed in `GallerySelectionContext.tsx` lines 120-131 that non-toggle gallery selections call `peerClearRef.current?.()` before replacing gallery selection. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed with zero errors, which also confirmed the `GallerySelectionItem` import/export surface and the `GenerationRow -> GallerySelectionItem` bridge mapping compile cleanly. `useSelection.ts` already exposes `mobileSelectedIds`, so no follow-up edit was needed for T3. As the required final verification, I created a temporary script at `.megaplan/plans/wire-image-selection-across-20260413-1425/verify_gallery_selection_bridge.js`, ran it successfully to assert the expected gallery/timeline/batch bridge wiring, and then deleted it.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed with zero errors after T1-T3, so there are no type mismatches in the bridge wiring. That compile pass covered the `GallerySelectionItem` import from `GallerySelectionContext.tsx`, the `GenerationRow` field mapping inside `useGallerySelectionBridge.ts`, the timeline bridge call in `useTimelineOrchestrator.ts`, and the mobile/desktop selected-ID branch in `ShotImageManagerContainer.tsx`.
